campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to hear a financial report from the local transit authority about their investments from March 2026, and they discussed a new policy that would prevent the city from allowing federal agencies to use city property for certain purposes. The council also addressed job openings in the city and ways to hire and keep good employees.
keyTopics: Transit authority's investment performance report for March
New policy restricting federal agency use of city property
City job vacancies and employee retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could voice their opinions on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced the ordinance (a new city law) and decided to skip the standard first reading process to move things along faster. The council then directed staff to add it to the consent calendar so it can be officially approved at the next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held - residents had opportunity to comment
New ordinance introduced to the council
First reading requirement waived to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the town manager to sign several contracts for construction services and professional agreements to move forward with various town improvement projects.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with multiple vendors including QLM, Inc.
Approved town improvement project planning and preparation
Ratified minutes from closed and public sessions held April 7, 2026

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve meeting minutes and discuss several projects affecting the city. Key items included plans to replace an old elevator at a terminal, deciding on companies to help clean up homeless encampments, updating an agreement with a digital billboard company, and approving a memorial event for fallen firefighters.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure update
Encampment Cleanup Services - request for proposals to address homeless camps
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel - amendment to billboard contract on city property
Light the Night Memorial Event - honoring fallen firefighters with city funding and donations

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear directly from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding which neighborhood grant requests would receive funding for the 2026-27 fiscal year, along with discussing attendance at neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Neighborhood meeting attendance review
Meeting minutes approval

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on the city's various boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comment.
keyTopics: Board and Commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunity
Online participation option for residents
Evening study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a private negotiation meeting about buying or selling property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The city manager and city attorney met with the Cupertino Union School District to discuss the price and payment terms for this piece of land.
keyTopics: Property deal negotiation for 10480 Finch Avenue
Price and payment terms being discussed with school district
Silicon Valley Hopper grant funding program overview

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also reviewed a new contract agreement with the city's firefighters union covering the years 2026-2029. One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs approved for Levi's Stadium during FIFA World Cup 2026
New 3-year contract agreement with Santa Clara Firefighters Union (Local 1171)
One agenda item withdrawn by city staff

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to approve the city's spending plans for the next several years. They discussed the budget for 2026-27, approved a new plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District area, and updated the rules for how the city council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed city operating budget for 2026-27
Adopted Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
Updated City Council procedures and meeting protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss several routine matters including director reports and meeting schedules. The main focus was a public hearing about a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots with a private street, allowing nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory dwelling units to be built there.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1-acre vacant lot into 9 residential properties on Old Trace Road
Proposal for 9 new single-family homes plus 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Creation of private street to serve the new development
Election of City Council Chair and Vice Chair
Project exempt from environmental review under state law
